name: Mount Kunlun
shortDescription: A mythical mountain in Chinese mythology, believed to be the dwelling place of deities and a source of immortality. It's often described as a paradise and the axis mundi, connecting heaven and earth.
description: Mount Kunlun is a central concept in Chinese mythology, representing a sacred mountain range believed to be the source of the Yellow River and the dwelling place of numerous deities and mythical creatures. It is often described as a paradise, a place of unimaginable beauty and abundance, and a crucial link between the mortal realm and the heavens. The mountain is particularly associated with Xi Wangmu, the Queen Mother of the West, who resides there and bestows immortality. The mountain is said to be guarded by fearsome creatures and surrounded by a perilous landscape, making it inaccessible to ordinary mortals. Myths surrounding Kunlun often involve heroes and immortals seeking enlightenment, longevity, or powerful artifacts. The mountain is structured in layers, with each layer representing a different level of spiritual attainment. It is considered the axis mundi, the center of the world, and a source of vital energy that sustains all life. The mountain's peaks are said to reach the heavens, allowing for direct communication with the divine. Its location is often ambiguous, existing both in a physical geography and as a representation of a spiritual landscape, embodying the connection between the human and divine realms. The myths and legends of Mount Kunlun have deeply influenced Chinese art, literature, and philosophy, serving as a powerful symbol of transcendence, spiritual aspiration, and the pursuit of immortality.

location.cosmological: Located in the west, considered the axis mundi connecting heaven and earth, a source of rivers and immortality.

sources.title: In Search of the Paradise: Middle Ages
sources.author: Jean Delumeau
